description_long: This object is a traditional stone water well with a circular stone base and a raised stone wall forming the well shaft. The well is topped with a pitched, shingled wooden roof supported by four vertical wooden posts, providing shelter and protection from debris. The stonework is composed of large, irregularly shaped blocks, giving the structure a robust and rustic appearance. The roof features overlapping wooden shingles arranged in a hexagonal pattern, with a slight overhang for rain protection. The well sits on a broad, circular stone platform made of concentric stone rings, which helps prevent erosion and provides a stable footing. There are no visible mechanisms for drawing water, such as a pulley or bucket, and the interior of the well is open and unobstructed.
description: A circular stone water well with a shingled wooden roof, supported by four posts, set on a wide stone platform. The well has a rustic, robust appearance with no visible water-drawing mechanism.
description_view_0: Front view showing the open well shaft, four supporting wooden posts, and the detailed shingled roof.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the stone wall and the overhanging roof, with the posts partially obscured.
description_view_2: Side view similar to the front, showing the open shaft and the symmetry of the roof and posts.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, with the stone wall and roof structure visible, and the posts mostly hidden.
attribute_surrounding_protective_structu: Stone wall approximately 1 meter high encircling the well shaft, and a wooden roof supported by four posts.
